sourceMovie.title: Tombstone
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Tombstone (1993) as a western theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The western and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Open Range
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tombstone clicked for you, Open Range (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in western and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Tombstone?
faq.answer: Try Open Range, Wyatt Earp, Silverado—each shares DNA with Tombstone while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Tombstone?
faq.answer: Tombstone (1993) is best described as Western, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Tombstone worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this western lane.
